Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: Goal: Create a process mining event log from provided ERP tables. Input: Several tables (Orders, Order Lines, Delivery, Invoice, Payments, Products, Resources). Output: A structured Markdown table representing the event log. Required Columns: Case ID, Activity Name, Timestamp, Resource/Actor, Other Attributes. Constraints: Case ID = order_id. Derive activities logically. Map timestamps correctly. Combine data using keys. Enrich with Resources/Attributes. Maintain chronological order. Document assumptions if data is missing/ambiguous.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Data & Define Events: Case ID: order_id (1001, 1002, 1003, 1004, 1005). Activities: 1. Order Created: From Orders table (order_date). Resource: sales_person_id. 2. Item Picked: From Order Lines table (item_picking_date). Resource: picker_id. Note: Multiple items per order = multiple events. 3. Order Dispatched: From Delivery table (delivery_dispatch_date). Resource: carrier_id. Note: Multiple deliveries per order = multiple events. 4. Order Arrived: From Delivery table (delivery_arrival_date). Resource: carrier_id. 5. Invoice Issued: From Invoice table (invoice_issue_date). Resource: billing_person_id. 6. Payment Received: From Payments table (payment_date). Resource: Finance System.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Process Each Order (Case): Order 1004: Created: 2024-01-04T14:15:00Z (SP-003). Picked: ITEM-001: 2024-01-06T08:00:00Z (WH-002). Missing Delivery? Looking at Delivery table: No entry for order_id 1004. Missing Invoice? Looking at Invoice table: No entry for order_id 1004. Missing Payment? No entry. Assumption: The process is incomplete for this case based on provided data. Include the events that exist (Order Created, Item Picked) and note the incompleteness.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 4. Refine Resource Mapping: Use the Resources Table to map IDs to Names for better readability in the Resource/Actor column. SP-001 -> Alice Brown. SP-002 -> Bob Smith. SP-003 -> Charlie Kim. WH-001 -> David Lin. WH-002 -> Eva Johnson. WH-003 -> Frank Miller. CR-01 -> GlobalTransport. CR-02 -> FastShip. CR-03 -> AirExpress. BL-001 -> Gina Carter. BL-002 -> Harry Lee. BL-003 -> Irene Park. Payment Resource: Finance System.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 5. Refine Activity Names: Order Created. Item Picked (Include Item ID in attributes). Order Dispatched (Include Delivery ID in attributes). Order Arrived (Include Delivery ID in attributes). Invoice Issued (Include Invoice ID in attributes). Payment Received (Include Payment ID in attributes).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. Review against Constraints: One row per event? Yes. Case ID = order_id? Yes. Chronological order? Yes. Missing data documented? Yes (Order 1004). Markdown format? Yes.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 8. Drafting the Content: Self-Correction on Resource: The prompt says Resource/Actor (optional but recommended). Using the Names from the Resources table is better. Self-Correction on Attributes: I should include relevant IDs in the Other Attributes column. Order 1004 Check: Confirmed missing. Order 1002 Check: Has 2 Delivery, 2 Invoice, 2 Payment records. Ensure all are included. Sorting: Group by Case ID and sort chronologically within that group.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 9. Final Polish: Item Picked. Since there are multiple items per order, should I create one event per item or aggregate? The prompt says: When multiple timestamps are available produce one event per activity if meaningful, or summarize if appropriate. Decision: In process mining, granular events are usually preferred to analyze parallelism or bottlenecks in picking. I will create one row per item picked. Similarly for Deliveries: One row per Dispatch and one row per Arrival per shipment.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 10. Final Review of the Plan: Create table. Fill rows based on logic above. Add assumptions section. Ensure Markdown. Check row count: 1001:7, 1002:11, 1003:7, 1004:2, 1005:7. Total:34 rows. Confirmed Order 1004 incomplete.
